title: helianthemum grandiflorum subsp. urrielense
intro: Helianthemum grandiflorum subsp. urrielense, also known as the Rock Rose or Spanish Rose, is a flowering plant species native to the Iberian Peninsula. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The Rock Rose is said to have health benefits such as wound healing, anti-aging, and immune system support, as well as potential applications in the treatment of fever, respiratory problems, and skin conditions. Herbal preparations of Helianthemum grandiflorum subsp. urrielense are often used in teas, infusions, and topical creams, and are sometimes combined with other herbs for enhanced therapeutic effects.
